info.title: UniFi Mobility API
info.description:
Proxy API for UniFi Mobility services exposed through the cloudservice-apis gateway.
Requests are authenticated at the proxy layer and forwarded to the mobility cloud.


## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Obtain an API key from the UniFi Cloud Platform with mobility scope
# 2. List your workspaces
curl https://api.ui.com/v1/mobility/workspaces \
  -H "X-API-Key: <your-key>"

# 3. List devices in a workspace
curl https://api.ui.com/v1/mobility/workspaces/<workspaceID>/devices \
  -H "X-API-Key: <your-key>"

# 4. Get full detail for a device
curl https://api.ui.com/v1/mobility/workspaces/<workspaceID>/devices/<deviceID> \
  -H "X-API-Key: <your-key>"
```

## Authentication

Every request must include the API key in the `X-API-Key` header.
API keys carry two layers of access control:

**App scope** — the key must be issued with `mobility` app scope.
Keys without this scope receive `403 forbidden`.

**Read / write scope** — controls which operations the key can perform:

| Scope | Permitted operations |
|-------|----------------------|
| `read:mobility` | All `GET` endpoints |
| `write:mobility` | All `PUT` endpoints (requires `read:mobility` as well) |

## Request Headers

| Header | Required | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `X-API-Key` | Yes | API key for authentication |
| `X-Request-ID` | No | Client-supplied trace identifier for end-to-end tracing. |

## Rate Limiting

**100 requests per minute** per API key across all mobility endpoints.
When the limit is exceeded, `429 Too Many Requests` is returned immediately.

## Response Format

All responses use a **unified format** with `httpStatusCode` and `traceId` as common fields.

### Success — single item

```json
{
  "data": { ... },
  "httpStatusCode": 200,
  "traceId": "abc-123"
}
```

### Success — collection (paginated)

```json
{
  "data": [ ... ],
  "total": 42,
  "offset": 0,
  "limit": 200,
  "httpStatusCode": 200,
  "traceId": "abc-123"
}
```

### Success — no content (PUT)

HTTP `204` with an empty body.

### Error

```json
{
  "code": "upstream_error",
  "httpStatusCode": 404,
  "message": "device not found",
  "traceId": "abc-123"
}
```

**Error codes:**

| `code` | HTTP | When |
|--------|------|------|
| `unauthorized` | 401 | API key is missing, expired, or invalid |
| `forbidden` | 403 | Key lacks `mobility` app scope or read/write scope |
| `rate_limit` | 429 | Rate limit exceeded |
| `upstream_error` | 400, 404, 500 | Validation error, resource not found, or service error |
| `server_error` | 500 | Unexpected internal error |

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description:
Workspace listing and admin management
A workspace represents a mobility cloud site (admin account).

tags.name: Devices
tags.description:
Device listing and detail
Returns UniFi Mobile Router devices registered in a workspace.

tags.name: Device Configuration
tags.description:
Write operations for device settings
All write operations require `write:mobility` scope and workspace **Admin** permission.

workspaceID.name: workspaceID
workspaceID.description: Workspace UUID. Obtain from API call: GET /v1/mobility/workspaces

deviceID.name: deviceID
deviceID.description: Device UUID. Obtain from API call: GET /v1/mobility/workspaces/{workspaceID}/devices

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Maximum records per page. Capped at 200.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response for all error cases.

WorkspaceSummary.description: A workspace (mobility cloud site) visible to the authenticated user.

AdminPermission.description:
Permission levels for the admin. Only mobility permissions are exposed.
The `umr` field is omitted if the admin has no role binding.

umr.description:
Mobile Routing permission level.
- `ALL` — can view and configure devices (admin)
- `VIEW_ONLY` — read-only access (viewer)
- `NONE` — no access

AdminSummary.description: An admin of a workspace, with only mobility permissions exposed.

permissions.description: Mobility permissions for the admin. `null` when the admin has no role bindings (e.g. pending invitation).

DeviceSummary.description: Lightweight device representation returned in the device list.

model.description:
Hardware model name.
- `UMR` — UMR Flex EU/US
- `UMR Industrial` — UMR Industrial EU/US
- `UMR Ultra` — UMR Ultra EU/US/ROW

state.description:
Current device state.

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `CONNECTED` | Online and communicating normally |
| `DISCONNECTED` | Lost connection to the cloud |
| `ADOPTING` | Adoption in progress |
| `ADOPTING_TIMEOUT` | Adoption timed out |
| `DOWNLOADING` | Downloading firmware update |
| `UPGRADING` | Applying firmware update |
| `RESTARTING` | Rebooting |
| `FACTORY_RESET` | Performing factory reset |
| `GETTING_READY` | Initialising after adoption |
| `RESTORING` | Restoring from backup |
| `NULL` | Record exists but adoption not started |
| `DELETING` | Cloud-side deletion in progress |

mac_address.description:
Primary MAC address in upper-case colon-separated format.
Empty string when not yet initialised.

DeviceLocation.description:
GPS location. This field is **omitted** (not `null`) when no GPS fix
is available — check for key presence rather than null check.

last_updated.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds of the last GPS fix.

allOf.description: Full device detail including WAN, cellular, WiFi, VPN, subscription, and GPS.

wan_source.description: Active WAN interface. Empty when no WAN connected.

wan_ip.description: Public WAN IP. Empty when not connected.

enabled_wans.description: Enabled WAN interfaces sorted by priority (index 0 = highest).

lte_signal_level.description:
LTE signal quality derived from RSSI (dBm).

| Value | RSSI range | Description |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `NO_SIGNAL` | < -111 dBm | No usable signal |
| `POOR` | -111 to -100 dBm | Weak signal |
| `FAIR` | -99 to -89 dBm | Moderate signal |
| `STRONG` | -88 dBm or better | Good signal |
| `""` | N/A | RSSI not yet reported |

cellular_data_usage_bytes.description: Data consumed in the current billing cycle, in bytes.

cellular_data_limit_bytes.description:
Data cap in bytes for the current billing cycle.
`-1` means unlimited. `0` is not a valid value.

uptime_seconds.description: Seconds since last boot. `0` when state is not `CONNECTED`.

host_address.description: LAN gateway IP. Contains WAN IP in `WANBRIDGE` mode.

device_mode.description:
- `ROUTER` — standard NAT router
- `WANBRIDGE` — WAN bridge mode
- `LTEPASS` — LTE passthrough

tx_power_level.description: Empty when Wireless record is not initialised.

vpn_profile_name.description: Empty when no VPN is configured.

vpn_status.description:
- `CONNECTING` — tunnel being established
- `CONNECTED` — tunnel active
- `DISCONNECTED` — configured but not connected
- `FAILED` — connection failed
- `""` — no VPN session

subscription_plan.description:
Active data plan. Empty when no subscription.

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `FREE_TRIAL` | Free trial |
| `1GB` | Monthly 1 GB |
| `5GB` | Monthly 5 GB |
| `20GB` | Monthly 20 GB |
| `2GB` | Daily 2 GB (QA/test) |
| `CLOUD` | Cloud plan (daily/yearly/3-year) |
| `""` | No subscription |

subscription_status.description:
Derived priority: FAILED > PENDING > ACTIVE > INACTIVE.
A subscription cancelled at period end remains `ACTIVE` until expiry.

location.description: Omitted (not null) when no GPS fix is available.

DeviceClient.description: A client associated with a device.

mac.description: MAC address in upper-case colon-separated format.

connection_status.description:
- `ONLINE` — actively connected
- `OFFLINE` — previously connected, no longer seen
- `BLOCKED` — network access blocked; `is_blocked` will be `true`

wifi_experience.description: WiFi experience score (0-100). **Omitted** for wired clients.

UpdateNetworkReq.description:
Partial update — only provided fields are applied.
WAN, IPv6, and InternetSource are not configurable via this endpoint.

UpdateWirelessReq.description: Both fields are required. Channel, TX power, and security protocol are not configurable.

PaginationMeta.description: Common envelope fields shared by all paginated collection responses.

total.description: Total number of records matching the query, ignoring pagination.

offset.description: Number of records skipped (echoes the `offset` query parameter).

limit.description: Page size applied (echoes the `limit` query parameter).

get.description:
Returns all workspaces visible to the authenticated user.
Only workspaces with active membership are returned.

**Scope**: `read:mobility`

403.description:
API key lacks required scope.
- Key has no `mobility` app scope
- Key has no `read:mobility` scope

get.description:
Returns all admins for a workspace with their permission levels.

The caller must have active membership. Only mobility permissions are exposed.

Admins are sorted: owner first, then active > pending > inactive > declined.

**Scope**: `read:mobility`

403.description:
API key lacks required scope, or the caller has no active membership
in the specified workspace.

404.description: Workspace not found or not a standard workspace type.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of devices in the workspace.
Each item contains identity and status fields only.
Use `getDevice` for full detail.

**Scope**: `read:mobility`

example.message: invalid limit: must be between 1 and 200

403.description: Key lacks required scope, or caller lacks Viewer permission on the workspace.

get.description:
Returns full detail for a single device, including WAN, cellular,
WiFi, VPN, firewall rules, subscription, and GPS location.

**Scope**: `read:mobility`

403.description: Key lacks required scope, or caller lacks Viewer permission.

404.description:
Device not found. Two scenarios return the same message
(to avoid leaking device IDs):
- The `deviceID` does not exist
- The device exists but does not belong to the specified `workspaceID`

put.description:
Updates the user-assigned display name of a device.

**Scope**: `write:mobility`

**Permission**: workspace **Admin** (Viewers receive 403)

204.description: Name updated successfully. No response body.

example.message: name must be between 1 and 32 characters

403.description: Key lacks `write:mobility` scope, or caller lacks Admin permission.

404.description:
Device not found. Two scenarios return the same message
(to avoid leaking device IDs):
- The `deviceID` does not exist
- The device exists but does not belong to the specified `workspaceID`

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of all clients associated with a device,
including ONLINE, OFFLINE, and BLOCKED clients.

`wifi_experience` is omitted for wired clients (`type = WIRED`).

**Scope**: `read:mobility`

400.description: Invalid query parameters or malformed device ID.

403.description: Key lacks required scope, or caller lacks Viewer permission.

404.description:
Device not found. Two scenarios return the same message
(to avoid leaking device IDs):
- The `deviceID` does not exist
- The device exists but does not belong to the specified `workspaceID`

put.description:
Partial update — only provided fields are modified.
WAN, IPv6, and InternetSource are not configurable.

**Scope**: `write:mobility`

**Permission**: workspace **Admin**

updateAll.summary: Update gateway IP and DHCP range

204.description: Network settings updated successfully. No response body.

example.message: host_address is not a valid IPv4 address

403.description: Key lacks `write:mobility` scope, or caller lacks Admin permission.

404.description:
Device not found. Two scenarios return the same message
(to avoid leaking device IDs):
- The `deviceID` does not exist
- The device exists but does not belong to the specified `workspaceID`

put.description:
Replaces the WiFi SSID and password. Both fields are required.
Channel, TX power, and security protocol are not configurable.

**Scope**: `write:mobility`

**Permission**: workspace **Admin**

204.description: WiFi settings updated successfully. No response body.

example.message: password must be between 8 and 63 characters

403.description: Key lacks `write:mobility` scope, or caller lacks Admin permission.

404.description:
Device not found. Two scenarios return the same message
(to avoid leaking device IDs):
- The `deviceID` does not exist
- The device exists but does not belong to the specified `workspaceID`
